leagueBriefing: Florida's Keagan Rothrock continued her dominant run with a series sweep over Missouri, while Arkansas showcased its power with a massive run-rule victory over Missouri State. Elsewhere, Drake and Utah Valley survived late-inning drama to secure narrow wins.

games.headline: Rothrock fuels sweep as Gators shut down Missouri
games.brief: Florida completed a perfect opening SEC weekend, leaning on another stellar performance from Keagan Rothrock to secure the series sweep over Missouri. Rothrock was efficient and immovable for much of the night, holding the Tigers scoreless until a late two-run single in the seventh. Offensively, the Gators did their damage early, highlighted by a Taylor Shumaker home run and timely hitting from Jocelyn Erickson to keep Missouri at arm's length.
bullets: Keagan Rothrock earned her third win of the weekend, pitching 15 of Florida's 25 total innings in the series.
Taylor Shumaker extended the lead in the fourth with her 11th home run of the season.
Jocelyn Erickson finished the series with a .417 average and five RBIs.
Missouri freshman Abby Carr drove in both Tiger runs with a two-out single in the final frame.
The sweep was Florida's first at home against Missouri since 2018.
games.context: Florida moves to 26-1 and remains unbeaten in SEC play while extending their win streak over Missouri to five games.

games.headline: Drake survives epic Colorado State rally in extra-inning thriller
games.brief: What looked like a runaway victory for Drake turned into an absolute battle in Fort Collins. After the Bulldogs built a commanding 8-2 lead, Colorado State mounted a furious six-run rally in the bottom of the seventh to force extra innings. Drake managed to regain their composure in the eighth, using a sacrifice fly from Pearson Hall to reclaim the lead and finally put away the Rams.
bullets: Colorado State scored six runs in the bottom of the seventh, capped by Lauren Stucky's two-run double to tie it.
Lili Gonzalez led the Bulldogs' offense with two doubles and three RBIs.
Drake's Pearson Hall drove in the winning run in the eighth inning with a sacrifice fly.
The Rams out-hit the Bulldogs 12-11 but stranded the tying run at third in the final frame.
Ashlynn Sheets earned the win in relief after stopping the Rams' seventh-inning momentum.
games.context: The win allows Drake to finish their Colorado road trip on a high note despite the late-game defensive lapse.

games.headline: McDowell slams Arkansas to dominant run-rule win
games.brief: Arkansas made quick work of Missouri State in Springfield, powered by a career day from Ella McDowell. The Razorbacks' offense was relentless from the start, putting up three runs in the first and five in the second before McDowell's grand slam in the third essentially ended the contest. The 12-0 victory marks the 15th time this season the Hogs have ended a game early via the run rule.
bullets: Ella McDowell went a perfect 3-for-3 with a grand slam and six total RBIs.
This was McDowell's second consecutive game with a grand slam.
Tianna Bell hit her team-leading ninth home run of the season, a two-run shot in the second.
Arkansas out-hit Missouri State 11-4 and did not commit an error.
Pitchers Payton Burnham and Lillie-Faye McWhorter combined for the five-inning shutout.
games.context: Arkansas improves to 22-2 and has now outscored their opponents 231-47 on the season.

games.headline: Wolverines hold off late UC Davis charge in Davis
games.brief: Utah Valley jumped out to an early lead and managed to withstand a gritty comeback attempt by UC Davis to secure a narrow midweek win. The Wolverines used a balanced attack to build a 6-3 lead heading into the bottom of the sixth, where the Aggies pulled within one run. However, Utah Valley's relief pitching held firm in the seventh, leaving the tying run stranded to escape with the victory.
bullets: Utah Valley's Maddie McSorley hit a solo home run in the third and added a stolen base.
UC Davis freshman Naomi Kalanetra homered for the second straight game.
The Wolverines racked up 12 hits compared to 8 for the Aggies.
Gia Felice kept the Aggies in it with two hits, two RBIs, and a stolen base.
Payge Armendariz earned the win, while Ella Miller locked down her first save of the season.
games.context: The victory moves Utah Valley to 15-8 as they continue their successful California road swing.
